title: Eulophia fractiflexa
taxonomy: Eulophia fractiflexa is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This species is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. Eulophia fractiflexa is also part of the genus Eulophia. Within the genus, it is classified as a terrestrial orchid native to tropical Africa.

intro: Eulophia fractiflexa, also known as the split flexure orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Eulophia fractiflexa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eulophia fractiflexa is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of tonics and syrups.
